title: The Land Of Dreams
content:
Awake, awake, my little boy!
    Thou wast thy mother's only joy;
    Why dost thou weep in thy gentle sleep?
    Awake! thy father does thee keep.

    "O, what land is the Land of Dreams?
    What are its mountains, and what are its streams?
    O father! I saw my mother there,
    Among the lilies by waters fair.

    "Among the lambs, cloth?d in white,
    She walk'd with her Thomas in sweet delight.
    I wept for joy, like a dove I mourn;
    O! when shall I again return?"

    Dear child, I also by pleasant streams
    Have wander'd all night in the Land of Dreams;
    But tho' calm and warm the waters wide,
    I could not get to the other side.

    "Father, O father! what do we here
    In this land of unbelief and fear?
    The Land of Dreams is better far
    Above the light of the morning star."
meaning:
William Blake's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Land Of Dreams"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
